figures.caption: Figure 1: Comparison of evaluation paradigms for LLM agents. (Left) On-policy evaluation requires executing the agent in the real environment, which is expensive and potentially unsafe. (Middle) Traditional off-policy evaluation learns a model-based simulator from offline data, but suffers from two fundamental issues: distribution shift between the behavior and target policies, and compounding error accumulated over multi-step rollouts. (Right) Our ADWMADWM addresses both issues simultaneously. Policy guidance injects πe into every denoising step to alleviate distribution shift, while the diffusion prior combined with action-aware denoising alleviates compounding error.

Table 2: Per-cell Spearman ρ between each estimator’s Ĵ and the real-environment ground-truth curve. ADWM is the only estimator with ρ>0 on every configuration (mean +0.82, min +0.67). CI95 and p-values from 2000 bootstrap resamples on the (Ĵ ,GT) pairs. Per-seed values and the underlying Ĵ / GT curves are in Appendix H.

Figure 3: Training loss curves across all four benchmarks. Total world-model loss LWM and its components converge stably within 50 epochs; see Appendix F.7 for full definitions and Appendix F.8 for the training procedure.

Figure 2: Architecture of ADWM. Offline trajectories are encoded by E into latent states zt, which are processed by a diffusion world model pθ through K-step denoising. A projector Gψ maps each latent to soft tokens õt that the evaluation policy πe can read in its own embedding space. πe plays two complementary roles: it steers the denoising process via policy guidance (dashed arrow, Section 4.2), and samples actions at conditioned on õt to drive autoregressive rollout (solid arrow). A reward head rρ predicts rewards r̂t used in the Monte Carlo value estimate Ĵ(πe).
Table 4: Per-module parameter counts in the ADWM world model (7.38M parameters total for dz=64).

Figure 4: Component ablation on three environments (avg-Ĵ Spearman ρ, single seed; five-seed trends in Appendix I). Each component dominates in a distinct reward regime: local CFG on sparse long-horizon tasks, continuation guidance on goal-conditioned trajectories, and the ψ adapter on linguistically rich state spaces. Removing any single component degrades at least one environment; full ADWM consistently achieves the highest ρ.
